message:
feat(workflow): Add workflow template loader and CLI commands

- Add workflow.Loader to load built-in templates from embedded files
- Support three-tier precedence: builtin < user < project overrides
- Add Source field to WorkflowTemplate struct
- Add 'ntm workflows list' and 'ntm workflows show' CLI commands
- JSON output support for machine-readable output
- Comprehensive test coverage for loader functionality
- Close child tasks bd-19fyf and bd-2r5ki for EPIC ntm-gd05

The 4 built-in templates (red-green, review-pipeline, specialist-team,
parallel-explore) can now be discovered via CLI. Users can override
templates in ~/.config/ntm/workflows/ or .ntm/workflows/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1377,6 +1377,7 @@ func init() {
 
 		// Configuration management
 		newRecipesCmd(),
+		newWorkflowsCmd(),
 		newPersonasCmd(),
 		newTemplateCmd(),
 		newMonitorCmd(),

@@ -11,14 +11,15 @@ import (
 
 // WorkflowTemplate defines a multi-agent workflow pattern.
 type WorkflowTemplate struct {
-	Name         string            `toml:"name"`
-	Description  string            `toml:"description"`
-	Agents       []WorkflowAgent   `toml:"agents"`
-	Coordination CoordinationType  `toml:"coordination"`
-	Flow         *FlowConfig       `toml:"flow,omitempty"`
-	Routing      map[string]string `toml:"routing,omitempty"`
-	Prompts      []SetupPrompt     `toml:"prompts,omitempty"`
-	ErrorHandling *ErrorConfig     `toml:"error_handling,omitempty"`
+	Name          string            `toml:"name"`
+	Description   string            `toml:"description"`
+	Agents        []WorkflowAgent   `toml:"agents"`
+	Coordination  CoordinationType  `toml:"coordination"`
+	Flow          *FlowConfig       `toml:"flow,omitempty"`
+	Routing       map[string]string `toml:"routing,omitempty"`
+	Prompts       []SetupPrompt     `toml:"prompts,omitempty"`
+	ErrorHandling *ErrorConfig      `toml:"error_handling,omitempty"`
+	Source        string            `toml:"-"` // "builtin", "user", "project" - set at load time
 }
 
 // WorkflowAgent defines an agent role within a workflow.
